sentences.text: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too.
One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club.
Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things.
We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too!
My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves.
There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing.
We never know what we're capable of until we try!

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
choices: the advantages of participating in extracurricular clubs at school
the importance of following teachers' instructions for academic success
why students should focus on building stronger friendships with peers
how trying new things leads to self-discovery and personal growth
the role of competition in motivating students to achieve higher goals
problems.explanation: 새로운 경험을 통해 자기 자신에 대해 많이 배웠고, 새로운 것을 시도하는 것의 중요성을 깨달았으며, 계속 배우고 성장하는 것이 흥미롭다는 내용이므로, '새로운 것을 시도함으로써 자기 발견과 성장을 이끌어낸다'가 주제이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
choices: Stepping outside one's comfort zone through new challenges helps us grow and learn about ourselves.
Teachers should assign group projects so that students can learn teamwork skills.
Academic achievement is the most reliable way to build confidence and self-awareness.
Students benefit more from individual reflection than from sharing experiences with others.
Setting too many challenges at once can overwhelm students and reduce motivation.
problems.explanation: 과제를 통해 자신에 대해 많이 배웠고, 새로운 것을 시도하는 것의 중요성을 깨달았으며, 더 많은 도전을 설정하자고 이야기했다는 내용에서, '새로운 도전으로 안전지대를 벗어나면 성장하고 자기를 알게 된다'가 요지이다.

problems.instruction: 필자가 이 글을 쓴 목적으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
choices: to persuade readers to enroll in a creative writing program
to share what was gained from the challenge assignment and encourage continued growth
to compare different types of challenges students can undertake
to argue that self-discovery is only possible through structured school assignments
to criticize classmates who did not fully commit to the challenges
problems.explanation: 필자는 과제를 통해 자기와 급우들이 배운 점을 공유하고, 앞으로도 계속 도전하고 성장하자는 메시지를 전하고 있으므로, '도전 과제에서 얻은 것을 공유하고 지속적 성장을 독려하려는 목적'이 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. <u>Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things.</u> We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
choices: Repeating familiar activities consistently is the safest path to self-improvement.
Only experiences involving physical risk can lead to meaningful personal discoveries.
Students learn best when teachers choose the challenges for them in advance.
The value of an experience depends entirely on whether it produces a successful result.
Challenging ourselves with unfamiliar experiences is essential for personal development.
problems.explanation: '새로운 것을 시도하도록 스스로를 밀어붙이는 것이 얼마나 중요한지 깨달았다'이므로, 낯선 경험에 도전하는 것이 개인 성장에 필수적이라는 의미이다. ①은 익숙한 활동 반복, ③은 신체적 위험만 해당, ④는 교사가 선택, ⑤는 성공 결과에 의존이라 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. <u>There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing.</u> We never know what we're capable of until we try!
choices: Growth and self-discovery are ongoing processes that never fully come to an end.
Self-knowledge has clear limits, and eventually there is nothing left to learn.
Excitement about learning fades quickly once the initial novelty wears off.
Students can only discover new things about themselves in a classroom setting.
Personal growth requires external validation from teachers and classmates.
problems.explanation: '항상 발견할 수 있는 것이 더 있고, 계속 배우고 성장하는 것이 흥미롭다'이므로, 성장과 자기 발견은 끝나지 않는 지속적 과정이라는 의미이다. ①은 한계가 있다는 반대 의미, ③은 흥미 감소, ④는 교실에서만 가능, ⑤는 외부 인정 필요로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 문장이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. <u>We never know what we're capable of until we try!</u>
choices: It is better to avoid challenges entirely than to fail at something unfamiliar.
People can accurately predict their abilities without needing to test them first.
Knowing our limitations beforehand prevents us from wasting time on impossible tasks.
Our true potential remains hidden unless we take action and attempt new things.
Success comes only to those who have a detailed plan before they begin a challenge.
problems.explanation: '시도하기 전까지는 우리가 무엇을 할 수 있는지 결코 알 수 없다'이므로, 행동으로 옮겨야 진정한 잠재력을 발견할 수 있다는 의미이다. ②는 도전 회피, ③은 시도 없이 예측 가능이라는 반대, ④는 한계 미리 파악, ⑤는 상세 계획 필수로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this ________________, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
problems.explanation: 앞선 내용(intro)에서 선생님이 과제(assignment)를 주었고, 결론에서 그 과제를 통해 배웠다고 하므로 assignment가 적절하다. argument(논쟁), assessment(평가), accomplishment(성취), arrangement(배치)는 문맥과 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different ________________ on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
problems.explanation: gain different perspectives on life = '삶에 대해 다양한 관점을 얻다'. regulations(규정), performances(공연), preparations(준비), permissions(허가)은 on life와 결합하여 적절한 의미를 이루지 못한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about _____________________________________. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
choices: avoiding situations that made us feel uncomfortable
returning to the hobbies we had practiced before the assignment
asking the teacher to give us easier tasks next time
focusing only on the challenges that we already succeeded in
problems.explanation: 새로운 것의 중요성을 깨달은 뒤 더 많은 도전을 설정하자고 이야기한 것이 자연스럽다. ①은 불편함 회피, ②는 이전 취미로 회귀, ④는 쉬운 과제 요청, ⑤는 이미 성공한 것에만 집중으로 글의 성장 메시지와 반대이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know _____________________ until we try!
choices: how others will judge our decisions
whether the results will satisfy our teachers
why our classmates chose different challenges
which subjects will be the easiest to study
problems.explanation: 원문은 '시도하기 전까지는 우리가 무엇을 할 수 있는지 모른다'이므로, what we're capable of가 적절하다. ①은 타인 판단, ③은 교사 만족, ④는 급우의 선택 이유, ⑤는 쉬운 과목으로 문맥과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all ________________ how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
problems.explanation: 경험을 통해 새로운 것을 시도하는 것의 중요성을 '깨달았다(realized)'는 문맥이 자연스럽다. denied(부인), ignored(무시), questioned(의문), avoided(회피)는 긍정적 결론과 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too!
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. ( ① ) My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. ( ② ) There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. ( ③ ) We never know what we're capable of until we try! ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 새로운 것을 시도하는 것의 중요성을 깨달은 직후, 그 결과로 다양한 관점을 얻고 재미도 있었다는 내용이 ①에 오는 것이 자연스럽다. 이후 더 많은 도전을 설정하자는 이야기로 이어진다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club.
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. ( ① ) Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. ( ② ) We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! ( ③ ) My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 급우들도 마찬가지였다(did, too)는 문장 직후, 그 구체적 예시(새로운 음식 시도, 글쓰기 동아리 가입)가 오는 ①이 자연스럽다. 'these experiences'가 이 예시들을 받아 다음 문장으로 연결된다.

problems.instruction: 주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.givenStart: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too.

chunks.A: Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things.
chunks.B: One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club.
chunks.C: We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too!

problems.explanation: (B) 급우들의 구체적 도전 사례 → (A) 'these experiences'가 (B)를 받아 깨달음 서술 → (C) 관점 확장과 즐거움이라는 결과. 'these experiences'의 지시 대상이 (B)이므로 (B)-(A) 순서가 필수.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하는 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
choices: All classmates chose the same type of challenge during the assignment.
The writer's classmates discussed creating additional challenges for themselves.
The writer felt that the assignment was a waste of time.
The classmates decided to stop challenging themselves after completing the assignment.
The writer discovered that only a few classmates benefited from the experience.
problems.explanation: ⑤번 문장: 'My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves.' 급우들이 더 많은 도전을 설정하자고 이야기했다. ②는 다른 종류의 도전을 했으므로 틀림, ③은 반대, ④는 도전을 멈추겠다는 내용 없음, ⑤는 일부만 혜택이라는 언급 없음.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
choices: The writer and classmates both learned about themselves through the assignment.
One classmate explored foods from other countries as part of a challenge.
The students realized the importance of trying new things.
The students gained different viewpoints on life through their experiences.
The writer believes there is nothing more to discover about oneself after this assignment.
problems.explanation: ⑥번 문장에서 '항상 발견할 수 있는 것이 더 있다(There are always more things that we can discover)'고 했으므로, '더 이상 발견할 것이 없다'는 ④가 본문과 일치하지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
choices: The assignment helped both the writer and classmates learn about themselves.
The classmates agreed they already knew everything about their own abilities.
Different classmates took on different types of challenges.
The students found the experience both enlightening and enjoyable.
The writer encourages continued self-discovery and growth.
problems.explanation: ⑦번 문장에서 '시도하기 전까지는 우리가 무엇을 할 수 있는지 모른다'고 했으므로, '자신의 능력에 대해 이미 다 안다'는 ④가 본문과 일치하지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
problems.summaryTemplate: By taking on new ____(A)____, the students discovered more about themselves and were motivated to keep ____(B)____.
problems.explanation: 새로운 도전(challenges)을 통해 자신을 발견하고 계속 성장(growing)하려는 동기를 얻었다는 내용과 일치. ①의 responsibilities/competing, ③의 hobbies/studying, ④의 routines/reflecting, ⑤의 resting은 원문의 핵심과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
problems.summaryTemplate: The students gained new ____(A)____ and agreed to continue ____(B)____ themselves through further challenges.
problems.explanation: 다양한 관점(perspectives)을 얻고 계속 스스로를 밀어붙이며(pushing) 도전하기로 했다는 내용과 일치. ②의 friendships/grading, ③의 protecting, ④의 credentials/testing, ⑤의 grades/rewarding은 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 did가 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out that my classmates <u>did</u>, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, while another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is to push ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives on life and had a lot of fun, too! My classmates and I even talked about setting more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can discover about ourselves, and it's exciting to keep learning and growing. We never know what we're capable of until we try!
choices: completed the assignment before the deadline
chose the same types of challenges as the writer
shared their experiences with the teacher privately
helped the writer finish the assignment on time
learned a lot about themselves through the assignment
problems.explanation: did는 대동사로 앞 절의 'learned a lot about myself through this assignment'를 대신한다. 즉, 급우들도 과제를 통해 자기 자신에 대해 많이 배웠다는 의미이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: I learned a lot about myself through this assignment, and I found out ①<u>that</u> my classmates did, too. One of them tried new foods from different countries, ②<u>while</u> another joined a creative writing club. Through these experiences, we all realized how important it is ③<u>pushing</u> ourselves to try new things. We gained different perspectives ④<u>on</u> life. There are always more things ⑤<u>that</u> we can discover about ourselves.
problems.explanation: it is important to V 구문에서 가주어 it의 진주어는 to부정사이므로 pushing → to push. ① 접속사 that(that절) ✓ ② 대조의 접속사 while ✓ ④ perspectives on life ✓ ⑤ 관계대명사 that ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: I ①<u>found out</u> that my classmates did, too. My classmates and I even talked about ②<u>setting</u> more challenges for ourselves. There are always more things that we can ③<u>discover</u> about ourselves, and it's ④<u>excited</u> to keep learning and growing. We never know ⑤<u>what</u> we're capable of until we try!
problems.explanation: it's + 형용사 + to V 구문에서 가주어 it은 사물이므로 감정을 유발하는 -ing 형용사 exciting이 적절하다. excited → exciting. ① found out + that절 ✓ ② talk about + V-ing ✓ ③ can + 동사원형 ✓ ⑤ 간접의문문의 의문사 what ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 that의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: There are always more things <u>that</u> we can discover about ourselves.
I found out <u>that</u> my classmates did, too.
We realized <u>that</u> trying new things is important.
She told us <u>that</u> we should share our experiences.
problems.explanation: ③의 that은 관계대명사(선행사 things를 수식), 나머지 ①②④⑤는 모두 접속사 that(명사절을 이끄는)이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 while의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: One of them tried new foods, <u>while</u> another joined a writing club.
Some students chose exercise, <u>while</u> others focused on hobbies.
<u>While</u> I was doing my homework, the phone rang.
She prefers reading, <u>while</u> he enjoys sports.
Some people like coffee, <u>while</u> others prefer tea.
problems.explanation: ③의 while은 시간 접속사('~하는 동안'), 나머지 ①②④⑤는 모두 대조 접속사('~인 반면에')이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 to부정사의 용법이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: They talked about setting challenges <u>to improve</u> themselves.
It is important <u>to push</u> ourselves to try new things.
It's exciting <u>to keep</u> learning and growing.
My dream is <u>to become</u> a great writer someday.
<u>To understand</u> yourself better, you should try new things.
problems.explanation: ④는 부사적 용법(목적: '향상시키기 위해'), ①②③은 명사적 용법(가주어 it의 진주어 또는 보어), ⑤는 부사적 용법(목적)이다. 하지만 ①②는 가주어 구문의 진주어, ③은 보어로 모두 명사적 용법이고, ⑤는 목적 부사적 용법이다. ④⑤ 모두 부사적 용법이지만, ④만 to improve가 challenges를 수식하는 형용사적 용법으로도 볼 수 있다. 정답은 ④: 나머지 넷(①②③은 명사적, ⑤는 부사적)과 달리 ④는 형용사적 용법(challenges를 수식)이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: I found out that my classmates do, too.
problems.explanation: 주절의 시제가 과거(found out)이므로 대동사도 과거형 did를 써야 한다. 시제 일치 규칙 적용.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: We all realized how important it is pushing ourselves to try new things.
problems.explanation: it is + 형용사 + to V(가주어-진주어 구문). 진주어 자리에는 to부정사가 온다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: My classmates and I even talked about to set more challenges for ourselves.
problems.explanation: 전치사(about) 뒤에는 동명사가 온다. to부정사는 전치사의 목적어가 될 수 없다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: There are always more things what we can discover about ourselves.
problems.explanation: 선행사 things가 있으므로 관계대명사 that(또는 which)을 쓴다. what은 선행사를 포함하는 관계대명사이므로 선행사와 함께 쓸 수 없다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: It's exciting keeping learning and growing.
problems.explanation: it's + 형용사 + to V(가주어-진주어 구문). 가주어 it의 진주어는 to부정사 형태여야 한다.

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.answer: found out that my classmates did
problems.explanation: find out + that절: found out that my classmates did(대동사). '급우들도 그랬다는 것을 알게 되었다.'

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: We all realized _________________________________ to try new things.
problems.answer: how important it is to push ourselves
problems.explanation: 감탄사절(간접의문문): how important + 가주어 it is + 진주어 to push ourselves. '우리 스스로를 밀어붙이는 것이 얼마나 중요한지.'

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: My classmates and I even _________________________________ for ourselves.
problems.explanation: talk about + V-ing: talked about setting / more challenges for ourselves. '더 많은 도전을 설정하는 것에 대해 이야기했다.'

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하시오.
problems.stem: 시도하기 전까지는 우리가 무엇을 할 수 있는지 결코 알 수 없다.
problems.answer: We never know what we're capable of until we try.
problems.explanation: We never know(결코 알 수 없다) + 간접의문문 what we're capable of(무엇을 할 수 있는지) + until we try(시도하기 전까지는). not~until 구문.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: I found out that my classmates _______ (do), too.
problems.explanation: 주절이 과거 시제(found out)이므로 대동사도 과거형 did를 사용하여 시제를 일치시킨다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: We all realized how important it is _______ (push) ourselves to try new things.
problems.explanation: it is + 형용사 + to V (가주어-진주어 구문). 진주어 자리에 to부정사가 온다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: My classmates and I even talked about _______ (set) more challenges for ourselves.
problems.explanation: 전치사 about 뒤에는 동명사(V-ing)가 온다. talk about + V-ing.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.explanation: it's + 형용사 + to V (가주어-진주어 구문). 가주어 it의 진주어는 to부정사이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: It's exciting to keep _______ (learn) and growing.
problems.explanation: keep + V-ing (동명사를 목적어로 취하는 동사). keep learning and growing에서 learning과 growing이 병렬구조.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: We gained different perspectives _______ life and had a lot of fun, too!
problems.explanation: perspective on ~ = '~에 대한 관점'. perspective는 전치사 on과 함께 쓴다.
